summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry and deploy FPVs, and also presented the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed fiber-optic FPV drones for the first time in Kharkiv since the start of the full-scale invasion, with one such drone striking the city and another hitting Perelyub village near the Chernihiv border. Ukrainian forces have used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar in Zaporizhzhia and to injure a 67-year-old civilian in Orikhiv. Ukraine's $880M drone line has reportedly turned Russian frontline movement into a "death sentence" in just 12 months, while Motor-G has become Ukraine's largest engine manufacturer, shipping 200,000 drone motors per month.
